# AiHomeCloud — Audit Task List v5
# Generated by: Claude Opus 4.6
# Date: 2025-07-26
# Status: All tasks OPEN — none have been applied

---

## How to use this file

Work through tasks in order. Each task is self-contained.
Mark a task DONE by changing [ ] to [x] after completing and verifying it.
Do not skip tasks — if a task has no findings, mark it N/A with a note.

---

## CRITICAL — Must fix before next device test

### TASK-001: copilot-instructions.md references non-existent `tasks.md` [x]
**File:** `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (lines 18, 24, 32, 242)
**Finding:** The file references `tasks.md` four times ("Read `tasks.md`", "update `tasks.md` after", etc.) but `tasks.md` does not exist anywhere in the repository. Every AI coding session reads this file first and is told to consult a non-existent file, causing confusion and wasted tool calls.
**Fix:** Either (a) create `tasks.md` with current priorities extracted from `TASKSv2.md`, or (b) replace all four `tasks.md` references with `TASKSv2.md` which does exist at the repo root. Option (b) is the minimal fix:
- Line 18: `See **tasks.md** for open work.` → `See **TASKSv2.md** for open work.`
- Line 24: `Read **tasks.md**` → `Read **TASKSv2.md**`
- Line 32: `update **tasks.md** after` → `update **TASKSv2.md** after`
- Line 242: `Update **tasks.md**` → `Update **TASKSv2.md**`
**Test:** `grep -n "tasks\.md" .github/copilot-instructions.md` returns zero matches. Verify `TASKSv2.md` exists.

### TASK-002: copilot-instructions.md uses wrong class name `CubieColors` [x]
**File:** `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (line 51, line 103)
**Finding:** Says `CubieColors` but the actual class in `lib/core/theme.dart` line 3 is `AppColors`. AI sessions generate code with the wrong class name, causing compile errors.
**Fix:** Replace all occurrences of `CubieColors` with `AppColors` in `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.
- Line 51: `Custom dark theme in **CubieColors** / CubieTheme` → `Custom dark theme in **AppColors** / AppTheme`
- Line 103: `Theme:** Custom dark theme in `CubieColors` / `CubieTheme`` → same substitution
**Test:** `grep -n "CubieColors" .github/copilot-instructions.md` returns zero matches.

### TASK-003: copilot-instructions.md uses wrong class name `CubieTheme` [x]
**File:** `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (line 51, line 103)
**Finding:** Says `CubieTheme` but the actual class in `lib/core/theme.dart` line 37 is `AppTheme`. Same impact as TASK-002.
**Fix:** Replace all occurrences of `CubieTheme` with `AppTheme`.
**Test:** `grep -n "CubieTheme" .github/copilot-instructions.md` returns zero matches.

### TASK-004: copilot-instructions.md uses wrong widget name `CubieCard` [x]
**File:** `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (line 104, line 159)
**Finding:** Lists `CubieCard` as a reusable widget but the actual class in `lib/widgets/app_card.dart` line 6 is `AppCard`. AI sessions import the wrong class.
**Fix:** Replace all occurrences of `CubieCard` with `AppCard`.
- Line 104: `CubieCard` → `AppCard`
- Line 159: `Reusable: `CubieCard`` → `Reusable: `AppCard``
**Test:** `grep -n "CubieCard" .github/copilot-instructions.md` returns zero matches.

### TASK-005: copilot-instructions.md says project is "CubieCloud" [x]
**File:** `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (line 1)
**Finding:** Title reads `# Copilot Instructions — CubieCloud` but the project name is "AiHomeCloud" (confirmed in `README.md`, `pubspec.yaml`, and `main.py` startup log). AI sessions may use the wrong project name in generated code and comments.
**Fix:** Line 1: `# Copilot Instructions — CubieCloud` → `# Copilot Instructions — AiHomeCloud`
**Test:** `head -1 .github/copilot-instructions.md` shows `AiHomeCloud`.

### TASK-006: copilot-instructions.md claims 5 bottom nav tabs — actually 3 [x]
**File:** `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (line 71)
**Finding:** Says `Bottom nav (5 tabs) + disconnected banner` but `lib/navigation/main_shell.dart` lines 188–201 defines exactly 3 `NavigationDestination` items: Home, Files, More. AI sessions may try to add tabs to non-existent positions or reference tab indices 3–4.
**Fix:** Line 71: `Bottom nav (5 tabs) + disconnected banner` → `Bottom nav (3 tabs: Home, Files, More) + disconnected banner`
**Test:** Count `NavigationDestination` in `main_shell.dart` matches the documented number.

## HIGH — Should fix before release

### TASK-007: copilot-instructions.md lists dead screens `MyFolder`, `SharedFolder` [x]
**File:** `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (line 64)
**Finding:** The Screens section lists `MyFolder, SharedFolder` as main screens: `Dashboard, MyFolder, SharedFolder, Family, Settings, StorageExplorer, FilePreview, FolderView`. In reality, `MyFolderScreen` and `SharedFolderScreen` are dead code not referenced in the router — they were superseded by `FilesScreen`. The screen list is also missing `FilesScreen`, `MoreScreen`, and `TelegramSetupScreen`.
**Fix:** Replace the screen list at line 64 with the actual screen files:
`Dashboard, Files, More, Family, StorageExplorer, FilePreview, FolderView, TelegramSetup`
Remove `MyFolder`, `SharedFolder`, `Settings` (replaced by More).
**Test:** Verify listed screens match files in `lib/screens/main/` that are actually imported by `app_router.dart`.

### TASK-008: copilot-instructions.md widget list outdated [x]
**File:** `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (line 159)
**Finding:** Widget list says `CubieCard, StatTile, StorageDonutChart, FileListTile, FolderView, NotificationListener` but is missing two widgets added in recent sessions: `EmojiPickerGrid` (`lib/widgets/emoji_picker_grid.dart`) and `UserAvatar` (`lib/widgets/user_avatar.dart`). Also `CubieCard` → `AppCard` (covered by TASK-004).
**Fix:** Update the widget list to: `AppCard, StatTile, StorageDonutChart, FileListTile, FolderView, NotificationListener, EmojiPickerGrid, UserAvatar`
**Test:** Compare listed widgets with actual files in `lib/widgets/`.

### TASK-009: KB storage-architecture.md references outdated `shared/` paths [x]
**File:** `kb/storage-architecture.md` (lines 11, 33, 77)
**Finding:** References `shared/` folder structure. The `shared/` directory has been renamed to `family/` with `entertainment/` split out as a separate top-level directory. These stale references mislead AI sessions reading the KB.
**Fix:** Update all `shared/` references to `family/` and add `entertainment/` where the directory structure is described. Specifically:
- Line 11: Update folder tree to show `family/` and `entertainment/` instead of `shared/`
- Line 33: Same
- Line 77: Same
**Test:** `grep -n "shared/" kb/storage-architecture.md` returns zero matches (or only the migration note if one is added).

### TASK-013: test_auth.py — no test for `/auth/users/names` endpoint [x]
**File:** `backend/tests/test_auth.py`
**Finding:** The `/auth/users/names` endpoint (defined in `auth_routes.py` lines 174–188) returns `{users: [{name, has_pin, icon_emoji}]}` but has zero test coverage. No test verifies the response format, the `has_pin` boolean logic, or the `icon_emoji` field. A regression (e.g., reverting to the old `{names: [...]}` format) would not be caught.
**Fix:** Add a test in `test_auth.py` that:
1. Creates a user with a PIN and `icon_emoji`
2. Creates a user without a PIN
3. Calls `GET /auth/users/names`
4. Asserts the response has `{"users": [...]}`
5. Asserts each user dict has `name` (str), `has_pin` (bool), `icon_emoji` (str)
6. Asserts the user with a PIN has `has_pin=True` and the one without has `has_pin=False`
**Test:** `cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_auth.py -q -k "user_names"` passes.

### TASK-014: test_store.py — no test for `icon_emoji` in add_user [x]
**File:** `backend/tests/test_store.py`
**Finding:** `store.add_user()` accepts an `icon_emoji` parameter (added in the emoji task) but no test verifies it is stored and retrievable. The existing `add_user` calls in tests only pass `name` and `pin`.
**Fix:** Add a test that calls `add_user("TestUser", "1234", icon_emoji="🏠")`, then calls `get_users()` and asserts the user dict contains `"icon_emoji": "🏠"`. Also test that the default (no `icon_emoji` argument) stores an empty string.
**Test:** `cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_store.py -q -k "icon_emoji"` passes.

### TASK-015: test_file_sorter.py — no entertainment-related tests [x]
**File:** `backend/tests/test_file_sorter.py`
**Finding:** `file_sorter.py` has `ENTERTAINMENT_SORT_RULES` (line 44) and entertainment-aware `_collect_inboxes()` (lines 59–67, 159–162) but `test_file_sorter.py` has zero tests for entertainment paths. A regression in entertainment sorting logic would go undetected.
**Fix:** Add tests that:
1. Verify `ENTERTAINMENT_SORT_RULES` dict maps expected extensions to subdirectories
2. Create a mock entertainment `.inbox/` with test files, run sort, verify files land in correct subdirectories (Music, Videos, etc.)
3. Verify `_collect_inboxes()` finds the entertainment inbox alongside the personal inboxes
**Test:** `cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_file_sorter.py -q -k "entertainment"` passes.

### TASK-016: flutter-analyze.yml missing `analysis_options.yaml` trigger [x]
**File:** `.github/workflows/flutter-analyze.yml`
**Finding:** Path triggers include `lib/**`, `test/**`, `pubspec.yaml` but NOT `analysis_options.yaml`. If lint rules are changed in `analysis_options.yaml`, the CI workflow won't run, so new lint violations could be merged without detection.
**Fix:** Add `- 'analysis_options.yaml'` to both the `push.paths` and `pull_request.paths` arrays.
**Test:** `grep "analysis_options" .github/workflows/flutter-analyze.yml` returns a match.

### TASK-017: backend-tests.yml missing `scripts/` trigger [x]
**File:** `.github/workflows/backend-tests.yml`
**Finding:** Path triggers include `backend/**` but NOT `scripts/`. Changes to `scripts/first-boot-setup.sh` are never validated by CI. There is also no `bash -n` syntax-check step for shell scripts.
**Fix:** (a) Add `- 'scripts/**'` to the path triggers. (b) Add a CI step: `bash -n scripts/first-boot-setup.sh` to catch syntax errors.
**Test:** Modify any file in `scripts/` and verify the workflow triggers. Check the `bash -n` step runs green.

## MEDIUM — Technical debt and coverage gaps

### TASK-018: Dead code — `shared_folder_screen.dart` [x]
**File:** `lib/screens/main/shared_folder_screen.dart`
**Finding:** This file is not imported or referenced anywhere in `lib/navigation/app_router.dart` or any other file. It was superseded by `FilesScreen`. It still exists on disk and adds confusion to the codebase.
**Fix:** Delete `lib/screens/main/shared_folder_screen.dart`. Verify no imports reference it.
**Test:** `flutter analyze` passes. `grep -rn "shared_folder_screen" lib/` returns zero matches.

### TASK-019: Dead code — `my_folder_screen.dart` [x]
**File:** `lib/screens/main/my_folder_screen.dart`
**Finding:** Same as TASK-018 — not imported or referenced in the router. Superseded by `FilesScreen`.
**Fix:** Delete `lib/screens/main/my_folder_screen.dart`. Verify no imports reference it.
**Test:** `flutter analyze` passes. `grep -rn "my_folder_screen" lib/` returns zero matches.

### TASK-020: Duplicate `_uptime()` helper in dashboard_screen.dart [x]
**File:** `lib/screens/main/dashboard_screen.dart` (lines ~1025 and ~1109)
**Finding:** The `_uptime(Duration d)` method is defined identically in two separate classes: `_DashboardScreenState` (around line 1025) and `_SystemCompactCard` (around line 1109). Both have the exact same implementation (days/hrs/mins formatting). This is code duplication — if the format needs to change, both must be updated.
**Fix:** Extract `_uptime()` to a top-level private function in the same file (e.g., `String _formatUptime(Duration d) { ... }`) and have both classes call it instead of each defining their own copy.
**Test:** `flutter analyze` passes. `flutter test test/screens/dashboard_screen_test.dart` passes.

### TASK-021: 14 of 15 Flutter screens have zero test coverage [x]
**File:** `test/screens/` (only `dashboard_screen_test.dart` exists)
**Finding:** Only `DashboardScreen` has a test. The following screens have zero test coverage: `files_screen`, `more_screen`, `family_screen`, `file_preview_screen`, `folder_view_screen`, `storage_explorer_screen`, `telegram_setup_screen`, `pin_entry_screen`, `profile_creation_screen`, `splash_screen`, `network_scan_screen`. This is 14 screens with no tests.
**Fix:** Prioritize tests for screens with user interaction logic:
1. `pin_entry_screen` — test tap-first flow, PIN-only-if-needed logic
2. `profile_creation_screen` — test emoji selection, name validation
3. `files_screen` — test 3 folder cards render, navigation
4. `more_screen` — test 4 grouped cards render, profile card, tailscale row
**Test:** Run `flutter test test/screens/` — new tests pass.

### TASK-022: No backend test for family/ migration in main.py [x]
**File:** `backend/app/main.py` (lines ~76–93)
**Finding:** The `lifespan()` function has a one-time migration block that moves `shared/` → `family/` and splits `entertainment/`. This migration logic has no test coverage. If it regresses, data migration on device update could fail silently.
**Fix:** Add a test in `backend/tests/test_endpoints.py` or a new `test_migration.py` that:
1. Creates a mock `shared/` directory structure with files
2. Creates a mock `shared/Entertainment/` subdirectory with files
3. Runs the lifespan migration logic
4. Asserts `family/` exists with the moved files
5. Asserts `entertainment/` exists with the moved entertainment files
6. Asserts `shared/` no longer exists
**Test:** `cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_migration.py -q` passes.

### TASK-023: No integration test job in CI [x]
**File:** `.github/workflows/`
**Finding:** There are only two CI workflows (flutter-analyze, backend-tests). There is no integration test that validates backend + frontend compatibility (e.g., API contract tests). `test_hardware_integration.py` exists but is always skipped on CI since it requires device hardware.
**Fix:** Consider adding a contract test job that:
1. Starts the backend with a test config
2. Runs a subset of API calls matching the Flutter `ApiService` methods
3. Validates response schemas match Dart model expectations
This is a design task — create a plan document or issue, not immediate implementation.
**Test:** N/A — planning task.

### TASK-024: CI Python version mismatch (3.12 vs device 3.11) [ ]
**File:** `.github/workflows/backend-tests.yml`
**Finding:** CI runs Python 3.12 but the Radxa Cubie device runs Python 3.11. No Python 3.12-only syntax was found in the codebase currently, so tests pass on both versions. However, a developer could inadvertently use 3.12-only features that pass CI but fail on the device.
**Fix:** Either (a) change CI to Python 3.11 to match the device, or (b) add a second matrix entry with Python 3.11 so both versions are tested, or (c) document the mismatch in `README.md` as a known divergence.
**Test:** If option (a): `grep "python-version" .github/workflows/backend-tests.yml` shows `3.11`. If option (b): CI matrix shows both 3.11 and 3.12.

### TASK-025: stat_tile_test.dart tests old vertical layout [x]
**File:** `test/widgets/stat_tile_test.dart`
**Finding:** The test uses a `Column(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min)` layout (Icon → label → value in vertical stack). If `StatTile` was redesigned to a two-column layout in the dashboard redesign, this test would be testing an outdated layout. Verify the current `StatTile` implementation matches what the test expects.
**Fix:** Read `lib/widgets/stat_tile.dart` and compare its `build()` method with what `stat_tile_test.dart` constructs. If the widget has changed layout, update the test to match. If the widget still uses a vertical column, no change needed.
**Test:** `flutter test test/widgets/stat_tile_test.dart` passes.

## LOW — Hygiene and cleanup

### TASK-026: AI session artifact files at repo root [ ]
**File:** Repository root — 11 files
**Finding:** The following files are AI session prompts / task planning docs that should not be in a production repository:
1. `FIX_DASHBOARD_STORAGE_TILES.md`
2. `FIX_EMOJI_AVATAR.md`
3. `FIX_TELEGRAM_SBC_ONLY.md`
4. `FIX_USER_PICKER_PIN.md`
5. `MASTER_PROMPT_v5.md`
6. `MASTER_PROMPT_v6.md`
7. `OPUS_AUDIT_PROMPT.md`
8. `REDESIGN_DASHBOARD.md`
9. `REDESIGN_MORE_SCREEN.md`
10. `SESSIONS_1_TO_4_PROMPT.md`
11. `TASK_13_TELEGRAM_LARGE_FILES.md`
**Fix:** Either (a) delete all 11 files and add `*_PROMPT.md`, `FIX_*.md`, `REDESIGN_*.md`, `SESSIONS_*.md`, `TASK_*.md`, `MASTER_PROMPT*.md` patterns to `.gitignore`, or (b) move them to a `docs/session-archive/` directory if historical reference is desired.
**Test:** `git status` shows no untracked prompt/session files at root.

### TASK-027: `logs.md` is a dev session log — should be gitignored [x]
**File:** `logs.md` (repo root)
**Finding:** Contains timestamped development session logs (e.g., "2025-07-25 — Trash overhaul"). This is a working-notes file, not project documentation. It grows with every AI session and has no value in the committed repo.
**Fix:** Add `logs.md` to `.gitignore` and remove from tracking: `git rm --cached logs.md`.
**Test:** `git status` does not show `logs.md` as tracked.

### TASK-028: `DevAgent.md`, `instructions.md`, `MASTER_PROMPT.md` at repo root [ ]
**File:** `DevAgent.md`, `instructions.md`, `MASTER_PROMPT.md` (repo root)
**Finding:** Additional session/prompt files at the repo root alongside the 11 listed in TASK-026. These are also AI session artifacts.
**Fix:** Include in the same cleanup as TASK-026. Either delete or archive.
**Test:** Files no longer exist at repo root or are in `.gitignore`.

### TASK-029: `critique.md` exists at both root and `kb/` [x]
**File:** `critique.md` (repo root) and `kb/critique.md`
**Finding:** Two files with the same name. The root `critique.md` may be a duplicate or draft. Having two creates confusion about which is authoritative.
**Fix:** Compare both files. If the root version is outdated, delete it. If it has unique content, merge into `kb/critique.md` and delete the root copy.
**Test:** Only `kb/critique.md` exists. `test -f critique.md` returns false.

---

## N/A — Checked, no issues found

- **Backend auth coverage**: All route endpoints use `Depends(get_current_user)` or `Depends(require_admin)`. WebSocket endpoints (`monitor_routes.py`, `event_routes.py`) use `decode_token()` with query parameter auth. No unprotected endpoints found (apart from intentionally public `/auth/users/names`).
- **`list_user_names` data leakage**: Returns only `name`, `has_pin`, `icon_emoji` — never PIN hash, user ID, or `is_admin` flag. Confirmed safe.
- **No hardcoded IPs**: `grep -rn "192.168" backend/` returns zero matches in source files.
- **No `print()` in backend**: All logging uses `logger.*`. Zero `print()` calls found.
- **No `print()`/`debugPrint()` in Flutter `lib/`**: Clean.
- **No `chmod 777` in scripts**: `first-boot-setup.sh` uses appropriate permissions.
- **JWT secret handling**: Default `"change-me-in-production"` is auto-replaced at module load by `generate_jwt_secret()` which writes to `/var/lib/cubie/jwt_secret`. Not a vulnerability.
- **No direct JSON reads outside `store.py`**: All persistent state goes through the store module.
- **No Python 3.12-only syntax**: No `match`/`case` statements or PEP 695 type aliases found.
- **`bandit` and `pip-audit` in CI**: Both run without `continue-on-error` — they are blocking checks.
- **Backend `@pytest.mark.skip` markers**: Only 1 found in `test_path_safety.py` line 228 — documented reason: `"Path sandboxing uses POSIX lstrip('/') — not applicable on Windows"`. Valid.
- **`QrPairPayload`**: Still in active use via `qrPayloadProvider` in `discovery_providers.dart`. Not dead code.
- **Dashboard redesign**: `_HeroStatusCard` (line 917), `_SystemCompactCard` (line 1039), `_StorageDeviceTile` (line 399) all present and correct.
- **Files screen**: Shows 3 folder cards (Personal, Family, Entertainment) using `AppConstants.familyPath` and `AppConstants.entertainmentPath`. Correct.
- **More screen**: Redesign applied with 4 grouped cards, `_ProfileCard`, `_TailscaleRow`, `_TrashCard`. Correct.
- **Onboarding screens**: `profile_creation_screen.dart`, `splash_screen.dart`, `pin_entry_screen.dart` all exist with correct route registrations.
- **No `CubieCard`/`CubieColors` imports in `lib/`**: Zero matches — all migrated to `AppCard`/`AppColors`.
